concept: Smell / To Perceive with the Breath
description: The act of olfactory perception, framed as a form of knowing that is intimately connected with the breath and the surrounding environment. It implies a subtle, atmospheric awareness.
rationale: The word 'hathoa' is built around the act of breathing. 'ha' uses the breathy 'h' to connect to the physical act of breathing (Pre-industrial). 'tho' uses the contemplative fricative to frame the act as a subtle perception (Buddhist). The vowel hiatus 'o-a' sonically represents an ambient scent ('o') coming into conscious, present-moment awareness ('a').
